solutions.title: Tutorial
solutions.content:
1431G - Number Deletion Game
The key observation is that Bob should always remove the minimum integer that is greater than $$$a$$$ on his turn (proving that formally is a bit complicated, but it works). So, if we sort the array $$$a$$$, after Alice removes the number $$$a_i$$$, Bob removes the number that was after $$$a_i$$$.
So, if we sort the array $$$a$$$, should we find $$$k$$$ non-intersecting pairs $$$(a_i, a_{i + 1})$$$ such that the sum of $$$a_{i + 1} - a_i$$$ is maximum possible? Unfortunately, this is suboptimal because when an element is deleted, the numeration of other elements changes. For example, if $$$n = 4$$$, $$$a = [1, 2, 3, 4]$$$, the optimal plan for Alice is:
erase $$$2$$$, so Bob is forced to erase $$$3$$$;
erase $$$1$$$, so Bob is forced to erase $$$4$$$.
How to handle these renumerations? Suppose Alice has deleted the number $$$a_i$$$ from the original sorted array, and Bob has deleted the number $$$a_{i + 1}$$$. Then, if Alice deletes $$$a_{i - 1}$$$, Bob is forced to delete $$$a_{i + 2}$$$, and so on. Thus, in $$$x$$$ turns, Alice may delete the left half of some segment of length $$$2x$$$, and force Bob to delete the right half of this segment.
It can be modeled with the following dynamic programming solution: let $$$dp_{i, j}$$$ be the maximum answer if Alice considered the first $$$i$$$ numbers and deleted $$$j$$$ of them (and Bob deleted $$$j$$$ of them as well). We can either skip the following number, transitioning from $$$dp_{i, j}$$$ to $$$dp_{i + 1, j}$$$, or delete some segment of length $$$2x$$$, transitioning from $$$dp_{i, j}$$$ to $$$dp_{i + 2x, j + x}$$$ (while adding $$$\sum\limits_{c = i + x}^{i + 2x - 1} a_c - \sum\limits_{c = i}^{i + x - 1} a_c$$$ to the value of dynamic programming). These sum queries can be performed with prefix sums in $$$O(1)$$$, so overall this solution runs in $$$O(n k^2)$$$.

solutions.title: Solution (Ne0n25)
solutions.content:

()
{
val
(
n
,
k
)
=
readLine
()!!.
split
(
' '
).
map
{
it
.
toInt
()
}
val a
=
readLine
()!!.
split
(
" "
).
map
{
it
.
toInt
()
}.
sorted
()
val p
=
IntArray
(
n
+
1
)
{
0
}
for
(
i
in
0
until
n
)
p
[
i
+
1
]
=
p
[
i
]
+
a
[
i
]
val dp
=
Array
(
n
+
1
)
{
IntArray
(
k
+
1
)
{
-
1
}
}
dp
[
0
][
0
]
=
0
for
(
i
in
0
until
n
)
{
for
(
j
in
0
until
k
+
1
)
{
if
(

[

][
j
]
<

)
continue
dp
[
i
+
1
][
j
]
=
Math
.

(
dp
[

+
1
][
j
],
dp
[
i
][
j
])

=

(
k
-

,
(
n
-

)
/
2
)

(
x

1.

)

[

+

+
x
]
=

(

[

+
2

][

+
x
],

[

][
j
]
+

[

+
2
*
x
]

+
x
]

[
i
+
x
]
+

[
i
])
}
}

(
dp
[

][
k
])
